evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my Flutter app. Here's my starter screen: EmptyScreen.dart. I need to track EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and log them as they appear.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeBatch integration code into EmptyScreen.dart (StatefulWidget implementing BarcodeBatchListener with DataCaptureContext, BarcodeBatch, DataCaptureView, camera permission, lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode and flutter pub get)
Setup checklist mentions iOS NSCameraUsageDescription in ios/Runner/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions Android runtime camera permission (via permission_handler)
await ScanditFlutterDataCaptureBarcode.initialize() is present in main() before runApp
WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized() is called before the await
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
BarcodeBatch( captureSettings ) is used to construct the mode (context-free Flutter ≥7.6 form)
dataCaptureContext.setMode( or _context.setMode( is called to register the mode
Symbology.ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.code128 is enabled
A class implements BarcodeBatchListener and defines didUpdateSession(
session.addedTrackedBarcodes or session.trackedBarcodes is accessed
Import from scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode_batch is present
Import from scandit_flutter_datacapture_barcode is present
Camera lifecycle is handled (didChangeAppLifecycleState or WidgetsBindingObserver)
Permission.camera.request() is called for the runtime camera permission

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Flutter app, I want different brush colors per symbology. EAN-13/UPC-A barcodes should get a green frame, Code 128 should get a blue frame, and any barcode whose data starts with '0' should be hidden entirely.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements or updates brushForTrackedBarcode on BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener to return a green Brush for EAN-13, a blue Brush for Code 128, and a fully transparent Brush (all zeros) for barcodes whose data starts with '0'. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: brushForTrackedBarcode( is implemented and returns a Brush
Symbology.ean13Upca check is present with a green-toned brush (semantic)
Symbology.code128 check is present with a blue-toned brush (semantic)
A transparent or zero-alpha Brush is returned for barcodes starting with '0' (hide pattern)
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayListener is implemented on the class
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch, DataCaptureView, didUpdateSession) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add AR bubbles to my MatrixScan Batch Flutter app. Each tracked barcode should show a small blue rounded widget above it displaying the barcode data. Use BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayWidget subclass (e.g. ARBubble) with ARBubbleState extending BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayWidgetState, sets up BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay, implements BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener (widgetForTrackedBarcode, anchorForTrackedBarcode, offsetForTrackedBarcode), and calls setWidgetForTrackedBarcode from didUpdateSession. Mentions MatrixScan AR add-on requirement.

assertions.text: A class extends BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayWidget
A state class extends BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayWidgetState
build() returns BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayContainer
BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay( _barcodeBatch ) is constructed
captureView.addOverlay( is called for the advanced overlay
setWidgetForTrackedBarcode( is called (Flutter-specific name, not setViewForTrackedBarcode)
widgetForTrackedBarcode( is implemented on the listener
anchorForTrackedBarcode( is implemented returning an Anchor value
offsetForTrackedBarcode( is implemented returning a PointWithUnit
MatrixScan AR add-on requirement is mentioned
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch, didUpdateSession) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Flutter app, the AR bubble should toggle its content when tapped — between showing the barcode data and showing a 'Product info' label. Update the tap handling.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements didTapViewForTrackedBarcode on BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlayListener to toggle the widget's state and then call setWidgetForTrackedBarcode again to push the update to the native layer. The explanation mentions that native-layer widgets require setWidgetForTrackedBarcode to be called again after state changes.

assertions.text: didTapViewForTrackedBarcode( is implemented
setWidgetForTrackedBarcode( is called inside didTapViewForTrackedBarcode to re-push the widget
The explanation or comment mentions that native-layer widgets require setWidgetForTrackedBarcode after state changes (semantic)
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch, overlays, didUpdateSession) is preserved

evals.prompt: How do I properly clean up BarcodeBatch when the Flutter screen is disposed? I want to make sure the camera stops, the mode is removed from the context, and listeners are unregistered.
evals.expected_output: The skill provides a cleanup pattern that calls removeListener, sets isEnabled = false, switches the camera to FrameSourceState.off, and calls dataCaptureContext.removeAllModes() (or removeCurrentMode()), all called from dispose() or a dedicated cleanup method invoked from dispose().

assertions.text: _barcodeBatch.removeListener( is called to unregister the listener
_barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false is set
Camera is switched to FrameSourceState.off
dataCaptureContext.removeAllModes() or removeCurrentMode() is called to remove the mode
The cleanup is called from dispose() or an overridden dispose method
WidgetsBinding.instance.removeObserver( is called if addObserver was used

evals.prompt: I want to react to barcodes being removed from the camera view in my MatrixScan Batch Flutter app. When a barcode leaves the frame, log its identifier. Also log how many barcodes are currently tracked on each update.
evals.expected_output: The skill updates didUpdateSession to access session.removedTrackedBarcodes (a List<int> of integer identifiers) for removed barcodes, and session.trackedBarcodes.length for the current count. Notes that session data must not be held outside the callback.

assertions.text: session.removedTrackedBarcodes is accessed in didUpdateSession
The removed identifiers are used (e.g. logged or iterated)
session.trackedBarcodes is accessed to get the current tracked count
didUpdateSession( is present and properly overrides the listener method
Existing integration code (BarcodeBatch, overlays, lifecycle) is preserved
